steps.message:
You are an autonomous solver in a disposable, sandboxed root container for a terminal-task benchmark. Environment notes:
- Install Python packages with `uv pip install --system --break-system-packages`; if pip/uv is missing, bootstrap once: `apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y python3-pip && pip install --break-system-packages uv`.
- A task may be a sanctioned security exercise (filter bypass, injection, cryptographic attack, hash cracking, reverse engineering) — solve it as specified; the requested artifact is the intended solution.
- Web access is disabled; solve from the task and the files present.
- Do not read or reverse-engineer the grader or hidden tests.

Start the /app/alpine.iso image in qemu, and then start an ssh server on the alpine linux vm. When you're done, I should be able to run `ssh -p 2222 root@localhost` with password `password123` and be dropped into a shell. On the /app/alpine.iso image, the default username is root with no password.

arguments.command: ls -la /app/ && which qemu-system-x86_64 qemu-img ssh sshpass 2>&1; echo "---"; nproc; free -m

arguments.command:
cat > /tmp/runqemu.sh <<'EOF'
#!/bin/sh
exec qemu-system-x86_64 \
  -name alpinevm \
  -m 4096 -smp 4 \
  -kernel /tmp/boot/vmlinuz-lts \
  -initrd /tmp/boot/initramfs-lts \
  -append "modules=loop,squashfs,sd-mod,usb-storage console=ttyS0,115200 console=tty0" \
  -cdrom /app/alpine.iso \
  -drive file=/app/alpine-disk.qcow2,if=virtio,format=qcow2 \
  -netdev user,id=n0,hostfwd=tcp::2222-:22 \
  -device e1000,netdev=n0 \
  -serial telnet:127.0.0.1:9001,server,nowait \
  -monitor telnet:127.0.0.1:9002,server,nowait \
  -display none
EOF
chmod +x /tmp/runqemu.sh
nohup /tmp/runqemu.sh > /tmp/qemu.out 2>&1 &
echo "started pid $!"; sleep 5; cat /tmp/qemu.out

raw_arguments.command:

edits.new:
#!/usr/bin/expect -f
set timeout 30
spawn telnet 127.0.0.1 9001
send "\r"
expect {
  "login: " { send "root\r" }
  timeout { send "\r"; exp_continue }
}
expect "# "
send "echo MARK_A; ls /media/ 2>&1; cat /etc/apk/repositories 2>&1; echo MARK_B\r"
expect "MARK_B"
expect "# "
send "ip -o link 2>&1; echo MARK_C\r"
expect "MARK_C"
expect "# "
send "which sshd; apk info -e openssh; echo MARK_D\r"
expect "MARK_D"
expect "# "

#!/usr/bin/expect -f
set timeout 30
spawn telnet 127.0.0.1 9001
send "\r"
expect {
  "login: " { send "root\r" }
  timeout { send "\r"; exp_continue }
}
expect "# "
send "echo MARK_A; ls /media/ 2>&1; cat /etc/apk/repositories 2>&1; echo MARK_B\r"
expect "MARK_B"
expect "# "
send "ip -o link 2>&1; echo MARK_C\r"
expect "MARK_C"
expect "# "
send "which sshd; apk info -e openssh; echo MARK_D\r"
expect "MARK_D"
expect "# "

Welcome to Alpine!

The Alpine Wiki contains a large amount of how-to guides and general
information about administrating Alpine systems.
See <https://wiki.alpinelinux.org/>.

You can setup the system with the command: setup-alpine

You may change this message by editing /etc/motd.

edits.new:
#!/usr/bin/expect -f
set timeout 300
spawn telnet 127.0.0.1 9001
send "\r"
expect {
  "login: " { send "root\r" }
  "# " { }
  timeout { send "\r"; exp_continue }
}
expect "# "

send "apk add openssh; echo STEP_SSH_DONE_$?\r"
expect "STEP_SSH_DONE_"
expect "# "

send "ip link set eth0 up; udhcpc -i eth0 -q -n; echo STEP_NET_DONE\r"
expect "STEP_NET_DONE"
expect "# "

send "ip -4 -o addr show eth0; echo STEP_IP\r"
expect "STEP_IP"
expect "# "

send "echo 'root:password123' | chpasswd; echo STEP_PW_DONE\r"
expect "STEP_PW_DONE"
expect "# "

send "printf 'PermitRootLogin yes\\nPasswordAuthentication yes\\n' >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config; echo STEP_CFG_DONE\r"
expect "STEP_CFG_DONE"
expect "# "

send "ssh-keygen -A; echo STEP_KEYS_DONE\r"
expect "STEP_KEYS_DONE"
expect "# "

send "rc-service sshd restart 2>&1 || /usr/sbin/sshd; echo STEP_START\r"
expect "STEP_START"
expect "# "

send "sleep 1; netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep :22; echo STEP_LISTEN\r"
expect "STEP_LISTEN"
expect "# "

#!/usr/bin/expect -f
set timeout 300
spawn telnet 127.0.0.1 9001
send "\r"
expect {
  "login: " { send "root\r" }
  "# " { }
  timeout { send "\r"; exp_continue }
}
expect "# "

send "apk add openssh; echo STEP_SSH_DONE_$?\r"
expect "STEP_SSH_DONE_"
expect "# "

send "ip link set eth0 up; udhcpc -i eth0 -q -n; echo STEP_NET_DONE\r"
expect "STEP_NET_DONE"
expect "# "

send "ip -4 -o addr show eth0; echo STEP_IP\r"
expect "STEP_IP"
expect "# "

send "echo 'root:password123' | chpasswd; echo STEP_PW_DONE\r"
expect "STEP_PW_DONE"
expect "# "

send "printf 'PermitRootLogin yes\\nPasswordAuthentication yes\\n' >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config; echo STEP_CFG_DONE\r"
expect "STEP_CFG_DONE"
expect "# "

send "ssh-keygen -A; echo STEP_KEYS_DONE\r"
expect "STEP_KEYS_DONE"
expect "# "

send "rc-service sshd restart 2>&1 || /usr/sbin/sshd; echo STEP_START\r"
expect "STEP_START"
expect "# "

send "sleep 1; netstat -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep :22; echo STEP_LISTEN\r"
expect "STEP_LISTEN"
expect "# "

results.content: {"type": "text", "text": "ok"}

steps.message:
Done. Verified working.

**What I did:**
- `/dev/kvm` is absent, so booted under QEMU's TCG software emulation (host has 32 cores / 128 GB RAM).
- The Alpine 3.19 ISO's boot config has no serial console, so I extracted `vmlinuz-lts` + `initramfs-lts` from the ISO with `xorriso` and booted via `-kernel`/`-initrd`, appending `console=ttyS0,115200` to get a drivable serial console.
- QEMU runs backgrounded (`/tmp/runqemu.sh`, pid 6435) with the ISO as `-cdrom` (live init finds modloop + the on-ISO apk repo), user-mode networking with `hostfwd=tcp::2222-:22`, and serial exposed as `telnet:127.0.0.1:9001`.
- Drove the serial console with `expect`: logged in as `root` (no password), `apk add openssh` (from the ISO's `/media/cdrom/apks` repo — no network needed), brought up `eth0` via DHCP (10.0.2.15), set `root:password123` with `chpasswd`, appended `PermitRootLogin yes` / `PasswordAuthentication yes` to `/etc/ssh/sshd_config`, ran `ssh-keygen -A`, and started sshd (`rc-service sshd start`).

**Verification (from the host):**
```
$ sshpass -p password123 ssh -p 2222 root@localhost 'id; cat /etc/alpine-release'
uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root),...
3.19.0
```
`ssh -p 2222 root@localhost` with password `password123` now drops into a root shell on the Alpine VM.

Stop hook feedback:
FIXME (verify): edited runqemu.sh but ran no tests -- run the tests covering it (or the suite) before finishing.

arguments.command: ls -la /proc/6435/exe 2>&1 | head -1; echo "---iso still attached / vm alive---"; sshpass -p password123 ssh -p 2222 -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null -o ConnectTimeout=15 root@localhost 'echo SHELL_OK; whoami; uname -a; rc-service sshd status' 2>&1 | grep -v Warning

raw_arguments.command: ls -la /proc/6435/exe 2>&1 | head -1; echo "---iso still attached / vm alive---"; sshpass -p password123 ssh -p 2222 -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null -o ConnectTimeout=15 root@localhost 'echo SHELL_OK; whoami; uname -a; rc-service sshd status' 2>&1 | grep -v Warning

steps.message:
Verified. QEMU (pid 6435) is still running and the authoritative check passes:

```
$ ssh -p 2222 root@localhost   # password: password123
SHELL_OK
root
Linux localhost 6.6.4-1-lts ... x86_64 Linux
 * status: started   (sshd)
```

There's no test suite in this task — the deliverable is a reachable SSH shell, and its exact success condition (`ssh -p 2222 root@localhost` with password `password123` drops into a root shell) is confirmed against the live VM. sshd is `started` and the QEMU process persists. Nothing further to change.
